## TASK 1: Set Supabase Secrets

Before anything else, push the API keys to Supabase secrets. The Founder has them in `DoubleSecret.env`. Read that file and set secrets:

```bash
# Read keys from DoubleSecret.env, then:
cd platform
npx supabase secrets set PINATA_API_KEY="<from DoubleSecret.env>" --project-ref <ref>
npx supabase secrets set PINATA_SECRET_KEY="<from DoubleSecret.env>" --project-ref <ref>
npx supabase secrets set PRINTFUL_API_TOKEN="<from DoubleSecret.env>" --project-ref <ref>
```

**CRITICAL**: Ask the Founder to confirm before running. Do NOT hardcode keys in any committed file.

## TASK 2: Real IPFS via Pinata Edge Function

Mock mode is DEAD. The Founder's words: "We need to ACTUALLY DO IT."

### 2a. Create Edge Function

File: `platform/supabase/functions/ipfs-pin/index.ts`

Follow the pattern from `social-oauth-callback/index.ts`. The function:

1. Receives JSON metadata (innovation data, ledger entries, etc.)
2. Calls Pinata's `pinJSONToIPFS` endpoint
3. Returns the real CID
4. Caller stores CID in `ip_ledger.entry_data` or wherever appropriate

```typescript
import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.168.0/http/server.ts";

const corsHeaders = {
  'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
  'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'authorization, x-client-info, apikey, content-type',
};

serve(async (req) => {
  if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
    return new Response(null, { headers: corsHeaders });
  }

  const apiKey = Deno.env.get('PINATA_API_KEY');
  const secretKey = Deno.env.get('PINATA_SECRET_KEY');

  if (!apiKey || !secretKey) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: 'IPFS service not configured' }),
      { status: 503, headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } }
    );
  }

  try {
    const body = await req.json();
    const { metadata, name } = body;

    const pinResponse = await fetch('https://api.pinata.cloud/pinning/pinJSONToIPFS', {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: {
        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
        'pinata_api_key': apiKey,
        'pinata_secret_api_key': secretKey,
      },
      body: JSON.stringify({
        pinataContent: metadata,
        pinataMetadata: { name: name || 'lb-ledger-entry' },
      }),
    });

    if (!pinResponse.ok) {
      const err = await pinResponse.text();
      return new Response(
        JSON.stringify({ error: 'Pinata error', details: err }),
        { status: 502, headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } }
      );
    }

    const result = await pinResponse.json();
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        cid: result.IpfsHash,
        timestamp: result.Timestamp,
        size: result.PinSize,
        gateway_url: `https://gateway.pinata.cloud/ipfs/${result.IpfsHash}`,
      }),
      { headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } }
    );
  } catch (err) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: err.message }),
      { status: 500, headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } }
    );
  }
});
```

### 2b. Update ipfsService.ts

Find the existing IPFS service in the platform code. Replace mock mode with a call to the edge function:

```typescript
// Replace mock CID generation with:
async function pinToIPFS(metadata: Record<string, unknown>, name?: string): Promise<string> {
  const { data, error } = await supabase.functions.invoke('ipfs-pin', {
    body: { metadata, name },
  });

  if (error) throw new Error(`IPFS pin failed: ${error.message}`);
  return data.cid;
}
```

Remove or flag all `MOCK_MODE` / fake CID generation paths. If a component checks for mock mode, make it always use real mode.

### 2c. Deploy Edge Function

```bash
cd platform
npx supabase functions deploy ipfs-pin --project-ref <ref>
```

### 2d. Test

After deploy, test with a simple curl or from the app:
```bash
curl -X POST https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/functions/v1/ipfs-pin \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <anon-key>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"metadata": {"test": true, "innovation": 1, "title": "Tab System"}, "name": "test-pin"}'
```

Should return a real CID resolvable at `https://gateway.pinata.cloud/ipfs/<CID>`.

The `send-transactional-email` edge function exists and works (Resend API key is already in Supabase secrets, DNS verified, from address: `noreply@lianabanyan.com`). It currently supports 5 email types: welcome, pledge_confirmation, credit_purchase, pledge_cancellation, milestone_update.

### 3a. Add `outreach` email type

Add a new case to the switch in `send-transactional-email/index.ts`:

```typescript
case 'outreach':
  subject = data?.subject || 'A message from Liana Banyan';
  html = outreachEmail(
    data?.recipientName || '',
    data?.senderName || 'Liana Banyan',
    data?.body || '',
```

### 3b. Create outreach email template function

Add to the template functions in the same file:

```typescript
function outreachEmail(
  recipientName: string,
  senderName: string,
  body: string,
  ctaText: string,
  ctaUrl: string,
  cueCardType: string | null
): string {
  return `
    <div style="font-family: Georgia, serif; max-width: 600px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 40px 20px;">
      <div style="border-bottom: 3px solid #e94560; padding-bottom: 20px; margin-bottom: 30px;">
        <h1 style="color: #1a1a2e; margin: 0;">Liana Banyan</h1>
        <p style="color: #666; margin: 5px 0 0;">A Worker-Owned Cooperative</p>
      </div>

      ${recipientName ? `<p>Dear ${recipientName},</p>` : ''}

      <div style="line-height: 1.8; color: #333;">
        ${body}
      </div>

      <div style="margin: 30px 0; text-align: center;">
        <a href="${ctaUrl}" style="
          display: inline-block;
          background: #e94560;
          color: white;
          padding: 14px 32px;
          text-decoration: none;
          border-radius: 6px;
          font-weight: bold;
          font-size: 16px;
        ">${ctaText}</a>
      </div>

      ${cueCardType ? `
        <div style="background: #f5f5f5; padding: 20px; border-radius: 8px; margin: 20px 0;">
          <p style="margin: 0; font-size: 14px; color: #666;">
            This message was sent via a <strong>${cueCardType}</strong> cue card by ${senderName}.
          </p>
        </div>
      ` : ''}

      <div style="border-top: 1px solid #eee; padding-top: 20px; margin-top: 30px; font-size: 12px; color: #999;">
        <p>Liana Banyan Corporation — A worker-owned cooperative</p>
        <p>1,662 innovations. 7 USPTO provisional applications. One cooperative.</p>
        <p><a href="https://lianabanyan.com" style="color: #e94560;">lianabanyan.com</a></p>
      </div>
    </div>
  `;
}
```

### 3c. Redeploy the edge function

## TASK 4: Printful API Integration — Edge Function

Create a Printful edge function for product catalog browsing and order submission.

File: `platform/supabase/functions/printful-api/index.ts`

```typescript
import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.168.0/http/server.ts";

const corsHeaders = {
  'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
  'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'authorization, x-client-info, apikey, content-type',
};

serve(async (req) => {
  if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
    return new Response(null, { headers: corsHeaders });
  }

  const token = Deno.env.get('PRINTFUL_API_TOKEN');
  if (!token) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: 'Printful not configured' }),
      { status: 503, headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } }
    );
  }

  try {
    const body = await req.json();
    const { action, data } = body;

    let endpoint: string;
    let method = 'GET';
    let requestBody: string | undefined;

    switch (action) {
      case 'get_products':
        // Get available Printful product catalog
        endpoint = 'https://api.printful.com/products';
        break;

      case 'get_product':
        // Get specific product variants
        endpoint = `https://api.printful.com/products/${data.product_id}`;
        break;

      case 'estimate':
        // Get shipping/pricing estimate
        endpoint = 'https://api.printful.com/orders/estimate-costs';
        method = 'POST';
        requestBody = JSON.stringify(data.order);
        break;

      case 'create_order':
        // Create order (confirm = false for draft, true for production)
        endpoint = `https://api.printful.com/orders?confirm=${data.confirm || false}`;
        method = 'POST';
        requestBody = JSON.stringify(data.order);
        break;

      default:
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({ error: `Unknown action: ${action}` }),
          { status: 400, headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } }
        );
    }

    const pfResponse = await fetch(endpoint, {
      method,
      headers: {
        'Authorization': `Bearer ${token}`,
        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
      },
      ...(requestBody ? { body: requestBody } : {}),
    });

    const result = await pfResponse.json();
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify(result),
      { headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } }
    );
  } catch (err) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: err.message }),
      { status: 500, headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } }
    );
  }
});
```

Deploy: `npx supabase functions deploy printful-api --project-ref <ref>`

**NOTE**: Orders created with `confirm: false` are DRAFTS. The Founder approval gate in the `print_orders` table means: platform creates draft orders, Founder reviews in admin panel, then confirms (sets `confirm: true`) to send to production. This is the "pulling the trigger" pattern.

## TASK 5: Wire Print Order Flow in Platform

Connect the `print_orders` and `print_batches` tables (created in Session 22) to the Printful edge function.

### 5a. Create print order service

File: `platform/src/lib/services/printOrderService.ts`

```typescript
import { supabase } from '@/integrations/supabase/client';

export async function createPrintOrder(params: {
  userId: string;
  productionLevelId: string;
  quantity: number;
  designData: Record<string, unknown>;
  shippingAddress: Record<string, unknown>;
  creditsCost: number;
}) {
  // 1. Insert print_order with status 'pending_approval'
  const { data: order, error } = await supabase
    .from('print_orders')
    .insert({
      user_id: params.userId,
      production_level_id: params.productionLevelId,
      quantity: params.quantity,
      design_data: params.designData,
      shipping_address: params.shippingAddress,
      credits_cost: params.creditsCost,
      status: 'pending_approval',
      requires_approval: true,
    })
    .select()
    .single();

  if (error) throw error;

  // 2. Deduct credits from user's balance (hold, not final)
  // This creates a ledger entry but doesn't finalize until approval

  return order;
}

export async function approvePrintOrder(orderId: string, approvedBy: string) {
  // 1. Update order status
  const { data: order, error } = await supabase
    .from('print_orders')
    .update({
      status: 'approved',
      approved_by: approvedBy,
      approved_at: new Date().toISOString(),
    })
    .eq('id', orderId)
    .select()
    .single();

  if (error) throw error;

  // 2. Check if batch threshold is met — aggregate approved orders
  // 3. If batch ready, create Printful draft order via edge function

  return order;
}
```

- Approve / Reject buttons
- Batch aggregation view (how many orders waiting for same product)

## TASK 7: Content Pipeline Automation

The `platform_canonical` table should auto-propagate to Cephas via Hugo data files. Create a build-time script:

File: `Cephas/cephas-hugo/scripts/fetch-canonical.js`

This script runs before Hugo build, fetches `platform_canonical` from Supabase, writes it to `data/canonical.json`. Then Hugo templates can use `{{ .Site.Data.canonical.innovation_count }}` instead of hardcoded values.
